subject: Human Resources
title: Psychological Safety
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on Psychological Safety.

hook.text: Google studied 180 teams. The number one factor in top-performing teams wasn't talent—it was psychological safety.

buildup.text: Psychological safety means people feel safe to take risks, speak up, and make mistakes without fear of punishment.

discovery.text: Amy Edmondson's research found that teams with high psychological safety learn faster, innovate more, and perform better.

twist.text: It doesn't mean being nice. It means being honest—where disagreement is welcome and failure is a learning event.

climax.text: Leaders create safety by admitting their own mistakes, asking questions, and responding to feedback with gratitude.

punchline.text: People do their best work when they're not afraid.

quiz.question: What did Google's Project Aristotle find was most important for team performance?
